ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for spicy mixed top shell cold dish. FOODON_03301737 (gastropod food product) is the closest defensible parent class covering cooked seasoned gastropod molluscs.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers cooked gastropods as a category. No specific code exists for spicy cold-dressed top shell preparation. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept found for spicy mixed top shell cold dish. SNOMED CT coverage of prepared gastropod dishes is absent.

main_ingredients: top shell (Trochus spp. or Turbo spp.)

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of spicy mixed top shell cold dish is approximately 200 g, representing a shared starter plate of seasoned top shell pieces with onions and chili.

nutrient_source.title: Whelk, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 15167); adapted with reference to USDA Foundation Foods raw top shell / conch (NDB 15148) and FAO/INFOODS SEA gastropod composition data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central; FAO/INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for spicy mixed top shell cold dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. USDA SR Legacy cooked whelk (NDB 15167) is the closest approved gastropod entry and is used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values for the seasoning components (chili, onion, fish sauce, lime juice) are minor contributors and do not substantially alter the gastropod-dominant macronutrient profile. Sodium is elevated above plain cooked whelk to reflect fish sauce and seasoning. All values represent estimated per-100 g composition of the dressed dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of shellfish and high-protein low-carbohydrate seafood — analogous estimate based on cooked molluscs and shellfish category; University of Sydney GI Database reference for high-protein low-carbohydrate seafood foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for spicy mixed top shell cold dish. Top shell is a high-protein, very low-carbohydrate mollusc. The University of Sydney GI Database consistently reports GI values of 0–10 for high-protein, negligible-carbohydrate seafood and shellfish foods. A GI of 5 is used as a conservative low estimate consistent with the gastropod protein-dominant composition. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (5 × 5.8 × 2.0) / 100 = 0.58, rounded to 0.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium from fish sauce and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Each 200 g serving provides approximately 960 mg sodium, representing about 42% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; patients with hypertension should consume this dish occasionally and request reduced fish sauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 5 and only 5.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g, spicy mixed top shell cold dish has a negligible glycemic load and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as a high-protein, low-carbohydrate seafood option.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium content offset by low saturated fat and beneficial seafood protein
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is low in saturated fat (0.2 g per 100 g) and provides lean marine protein, which is cardioprotective, but the high sodium content from fish sauce warrants caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease who are managing blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Mixed Top Shell Cold Dish (凉拌辣海螺, Liang Ban La Hai Luo) is a Vietnamese-influenced cold seafood appetiser served in Singapore. It features top shell (Trochus or Turbo species), a marine gastropod mollusc, that is blanched or briefly cooked and then cold-dressed in a tangy, spicy Vietnamese-style marinade comprising fish sauce, lime juice, chili, garlic, sugar, and sliced onions. The dish is served chilled, giving it a firm, slightly chewy texture with a bright, pungent, and spicy flavour profile. It is a popular starter at Vietnamese-Singaporean restaurants such as Viet'Spice. Nutritionally, the dish is high in lean marine protein (approximately 18 g per 100 g), very low in fat (0.9 g per 100 g), and low in carbohydrates (5.8 g per 100 g), making it suitable for high-protein dietary patterns. The glycemic index is very low (estimated GI ~5), making it appropriate for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is elevated due to fish sauce seasoning (approximately 480 mg per 100 g), which is a consideration for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. It is not suitable for shellfish-allergic individuals. Top shell is a good source of iron, vitamin B12, phosphorus, and zinc, reflecting the typical micronutrient profile of marine gastropods.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for spicy mixed top shell cold dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked whelk (NDB 15167), the closest approved gastropod analogue, with sodium adjusted upward to reflect fish sauce seasoning. GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database category for high-protein low-carbohydrate seafood. Confidence is medium rather than low because the gastropod protein-dominant composition is well-characterised in approved sources and the adaptation is scientifically defensible.
notes: Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy whelk entry and is not reliably estimated for this preparation. Vitamin B1 (vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Vitamin B2 (vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Vitamin B3 (vitamin_b3_niacin_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Vitamin B6 (vitamin_b6_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Folate (folate_mcg_dfe) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0 as marine gastropods contain no industrially produced trans fats; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central whelk entry. Halal status is marked false as the dish contains fish sauce which may include non-halal processing; verification with the specific restaurant is required. The dish is listed as BB2 on the Viet'Spice menu at Circular Road, Singapore, priced at SGD 16.90. GI set to 5 as an analogous estimate for a high-protein, very low-carbohydrate gastropod mollusc dish; gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55).
